stemHtml: Which of the following statements concerning aviation safety culture is correct?

options.a: In an open culture, the highest good is the cumulative well-being of the society as a whole.
options.b: A just culture is a culture in which errors made in flight are never prosecuted in order to encourage pilots to openly communicate them.
options.c: An informed culture actively collects, analyses and distributes safety-related data.
options.d: In a flexible culture, committed errors can be voiced without risking negative consequences for oneself.
